campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial report from the regional transportation authority covering March 2026 investments, passed a new policy restricting how the city's buildings and property can be used by federal agencies, and discussed efforts to recruit and keep city employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority investment report for March 2026
New policy limiting federal agency use of city-owned buildings and property
City recruitment and retention strategy for municipal staff positions

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could speak about a proposed new rule (ordinance). After listening to public input, the council decided to move forward with the ordinance and skip the usual first reading process so it can be voted on and approved at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite adoption
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Council moved ordinance to consent calendar for streamlined approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting notes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the town manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed city's financial and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other construction companies
Approved agreements for professional services (details partially unclear from agenda)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several items including routine meeting minutes and a project to replace an old elevator at a terminal facility. They also discussed plans to clean up homeless encampments around the city, approved a digital advertising deal with Clear Channel Outdoor, and authorized spending city funds for a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade
Encampment Cleanup Services - RFP for homeless camp removal
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel - large format ads on city property
Light the Night Memorial Event - honoring fallen firefighters with city funding

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to review and approve meeting minutes, hear from residents during public comment, and discuss how to distribute neighborhood grant money for the 2026-27 fiscal year. The council also reviewed attendance at neighborhood meetings and heard updates from committees and staff members.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comments from residents
Neighborhood meeting attendance and participation
Meeting minutes approval
Committee and staff updates

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comments.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
Public comment opportunity (in-person and online)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council met to discuss buying or selling a piece of property on Finch Avenue with the local school district, with the city manager and attorney leading negotiations on price and payment terms. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans for getting grant money to fund it in the future.
keyTopics: Property deal with Cupertino Union School District at 10480 Finch Avenue
Negotiations over purchase price and payment terms
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events happening in the city. The council also approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters that will run through 2029.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026
New three-year contract agreement with Santa Clara firefighters (2026-2029)
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss money plans for the next several years, including approving a five-year construction and infrastructure budget for 2027-2031 and reviewing the upcoming year's spending plan. The council also approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the council runs its meetings.
keyTopics: Five-year capital budget (2027-2031) for city construction and infrastructure projects
Fiscal year 2026-27 operating budget presentation and approval
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adoption
Updates to City Council meeting procedures and rules

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on April 29, 2026 to discuss several routine matters and a significant development proposal for Old Trace Road. The main item was a request to divide a vacant 1-acre lot into 9 residential lots and build 9 new single-family homes plus 7 smaller accessory units on the property.
keyTopics: Proposed subdivision of 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots
Plan to build 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Environmental review exemption approved under state law (AB 130)
Council leadership elections for Chair and Vice Chair positions
